questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $f(x)=\left(20-x^4\right)^{1 / 4} \quad$ for $\quad 0 < x < \sqrt{5}$, then $f\left(f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\left(20-x^4\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\ f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) & =\left(20-\frac{1}{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}=\left(\frac{319}{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\ f\left[f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right] & =f\left[\left(\frac{319}{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}\right] \\ & =\left(20-\frac{319}{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\ & =\left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{1}{2}=2^{-1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the functions $f$ and $g$ are defined by $f(x)=3 x-4, \quad g(x)=2+3 x \quad$ for $\quad x \in R$ respective, then $g^{-1}\left(f^{-1}(5)\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=3 x-4=y \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 x=y+4 \Rightarrow x=\frac{y+4}{3} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f^{-1}(y)=\frac{y+4}{3} \\
& \text { and } \quad g(x)=2+3 x=z \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 x=z-2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{z-2}{3} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad g^{-1}(z)=\frac{z-2}{3} \\
& \therefore \quad g^{-1}\left(f^{-1}(5)\right)=g^{-1}\left[\frac{5+4}{3}\right]=g^{-1}[3] \\
& =\frac{3-2}{3}=\frac{1}{3} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The domain of the function $\mathrm{f}(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{|x|+5}{x^2+1}\right)$ is $(-\infty,-a] \cup[a, \infty)$. Then $a$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{|x|+5}{x^2+1}\right)$
$\mathrm{f}(x)$ is defined, if
$\begin{aligned}
& -1 \leq \frac{|x|+5}{x^2+1} \leq 1 \\
& \Rightarrow 0 \leq \frac{|x|+5}{x^2+1} \leq 1 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2-|x|-4 \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow\left(|x|-\frac{1-\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)\left(|x|-\frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{2}\right) \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow|x| \geq \frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{2} \\
& \text { or }|x| \leq \frac{1-\sqrt{17}}{2}, \text { which is not possible } \\
& \Rightarrow x \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{2}\right] \cup\left[\frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{2}, \infty\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=\frac{1+\sqrt{17}}{2}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The function $f: R \rightarrow R$ is defined by $f(x)=\cos ^2 x+\sin ^4 x$ for $x \in R$, then $f(R)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\cos ^2 x+\sin ^4 x=1-\sin ^2 x+\sin ^4 x \\
& =1-\sin ^2 x\left(1-\sin ^2 x\right)=1-\sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x \\
& =1-\frac{1}{4} \sin ^2 2 x
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\text { Here, } 0 \leq \sin ^2 2 x \leq 1 \Rightarrow 0 \leq \frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{4} \leq \frac{1}{4}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad 0 \geq-\frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{4} \geq-\frac{1}{4} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 1 \geq 1-\frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{4} \geq-\frac{1}{4} \\
& 1 \geq 1-\frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{4} \geq-\frac{1}{4}+1 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 1 \geq 1-\frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{4} \geq \frac{3}{4} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Range of $f=\left[\frac{3}{4}, 1\right]$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The function $f: R \rightarrow R$ is defined by $f(x)=3^{-x}$. Observe the following statements of it
I. $f$ is one-one
II. $f$ is onto
III. $f$ is a decreasing function
Out of these, true statement are
questions.solution:
Since, $f: R \rightarrow R$ such that $f(x)=3^{-x}$.
Let $y_1$ and $y_2$ be two elements of $f(x)$ such that
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y_1=y_2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3^{-x_1}=3^{-x_2} \Rightarrow x_1=x_2 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Since, if two images are equal, then their elements are equal, therefore it is one-one function
Let
$$
y=3^{-x}
$$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$\frac{d y}{d x}=-3^{-x} \log 3 < 0$ for every value of $x$
$\therefore$ It is decreasing function
$\therefore$ Statement I and II are true.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer not exceeding $x$, then the values of $x$ satisfying $[x]^2-7[x]+12 \leq 0$ are
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Given equation, } \\ & \qquad[x]^2-7[x]+12 \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad[x]^2-4[x]-3[x]+12 \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad([x]-4)([x]-3) \leq 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad[x] \in[3,4] \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x \in[3,5) .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\quad e^{f(x)}=\frac{10+x}{10-x}, x \in(-10,10) \quad$ and $f(x)=k f\left(\frac{200 x}{100+x^2}\right)$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$e^{f(x)}=\frac{10+x}{10-x}$
$f(x)=\log \frac{10+x}{10-x}$
Given that $f(x)=k f\left(\frac{200 x}{100+x^2}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \log \frac{10+x}{10-x}=k \cdot \log \left\{\frac{10+\frac{200 x}{100+x^2}}{10-\frac{200 x}{100+x^2}}\right\}$
$=k \log \left\{\frac{1000+10 x^2+200 x}{1000+10 x^2-200 x}\right\}$
$=k \log \left\{\frac{x^2+100+20 x}{x^2+100-20 x}\right\}$
$=k \log \left\{\frac{(x+10)^2}{(10-x)^2}\right\}$
$=2 k \log \frac{10+x}{10-x}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \log \frac{10+x}{10-x}=2 k \log \frac{10+x}{10-x}$
$\therefore \quad 2 k=1 \Rightarrow k=\frac{1}{2}=0.5$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The function $f: C \rightarrow C$ defined by $f(x)=\frac{a x+d}{c x+d}$ for $x \in C$ where $b d \neq 0$ reduces to a constant function, if
questions.solution: Now, take option (c) i.e., $a d=b c$ $\Rightarrow \quad \frac{a}{b}=\frac{c}{d}=k$ (say) From equation (i), $\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\frac{b k x+b}{d k x+d} \\ & =\frac{b(k x+1)}{d(k x+1)}=\frac{b}{d}=\text { constant } \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If the function $f:[a, b] \rightarrow\left[-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}, \frac{1}{2}\right]$ defined by
$$
f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & 1 & 1 \\
1 & 1+\sin x & 1 \\
1+\cos x & 1 & 1
\end{array}\right|
$$
is one-one and onto, then
questions.solution:
We have, $f:(a, b) \rightarrow\left[\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{4}, \frac{1}{2}\right]$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & 1 & 1 \\
1 & 1+\sin x & 1 \\
1+\cos x & 1 & 1
\end{array}\right| \\
& f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & 0 & 0 \\
1 & \sin x & 0 \\
1+\cos x & -\cos x & -\cos x
\end{array}\right| \\
& \quad\left[C_3 \rightarrow C_3-C_1, C_2 \rightarrow C_2-C_1\right] \\
& f(x)=-\sin x \cos x \\
& f(x)=-\frac{1}{2} \sin 2 x \\
& f(x) \in\left[\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}, \frac{1}{2}\right]
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& \therefore & -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} & \leq \frac{-1}{2} \sin 2 x \leq \frac{1}{2} \\
\Rightarrow & & -1 & \leq \sin 2 x \leq \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\
\Rightarrow & -\frac{\pi}{4} & \leq x \leq \frac{\pi}{6} \\
& \therefore & a & =-\frac{\pi}{4}, b=\frac{\pi}{6}
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $[x]$ represent greatest integer $\leq x$ and $[\alpha, \beta]$ is the set of all real values of $x$ for which the real function $f(x)=\frac{\sqrt{3+x}+\sqrt{3-x}}{\sqrt{[x]+2}}$ is defined, then $f^2(\alpha+1)+5 f^2(\beta)=$
questions.solution:
The given function $f(x)=\frac{\sqrt{3+x}+\sqrt{3-x}}{\sqrt{[x]+2}}$,
then function $f$ is defined if
$3+x \geq 0,3-x \geq 0$ and $[x]+2>0$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad x \geq-3, x \leq 3 \text { and }[x]>-2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x \geq-3, x \leq 3 \text { and } x \in[-1, \infty) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x \in[-1,3]=[\alpha, \beta] \text { (given) } \\ & \text { So, } \alpha=-1 \text { and } \beta=3\end{aligned}$
$\therefore f^2(\alpha+1)+5 f^2(\beta)=f^2(0)+5 f^2(3)$
$\begin{aligned} & =\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^2+5\left(\frac{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{0}}{\sqrt{5}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{4 \times 3}{2}\right)+5\left(\frac{6}{5}\right) \\ & =6+6=12\end{aligned}$
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: The domain of the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>sec</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mi>h</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mn>5</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Given that

fx=sec-13x-4+tanh-1x+35

Domain of sec-1x

x ∈ (-∞,-1] ∪[1,∞)

sec-13x-4 is

⇒3x-4 ∈(-∞,-1] ∪[1,∞)

⇒3x ∈(-∞,3]∪[5,∞)

⇒x ∈(-∞,1]∪53,∞   ...(I)

We know that domain of tanh-1x is -1,1

⇒x+35 ∈-1,1

⇒x+3 ∈ -5,5

⇒x ∈-8,2   ...(II)

From I & (II)

(-8,1]∪53,2.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $f: R \rightarrow R$ is defined by $f(x)=\left[\frac{x}{5}\right]$ for $x \in R$, where $[y]$ denotes the greatest integer not exceeding $y$, then $\{f(x):|x| < 71\}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $f: R \rightarrow R$ and $f(x)=\left[\frac{x}{5}\right]$
Also,
$\begin{aligned}
& \{f(x):|x| < 71\}=\{f(x):-71 < x < 71\} \\
& =\left\{\left[\frac{x}{5}\right]:-71 < x < 71\right\} \\
& \left\{\left[\frac{-70-0.1}{5}\right], \ldots,\left[\frac{-70}{5}\right], \ldots,\left[\frac{0}{5}\right],\right. \\
& \left.\ldots,\left[\frac{65}{5}\right], \ldots,\left[\frac{70}{5}\right], \ldots,\left[\frac{70+0.999}{h}\right]\right\} \\
& =\{-15,-14, \ldots, 0, \ldots, 13,14\}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A function $f: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ is such that $f(\mathrm{l})=2$ and $f(x+y)=f(x) \cdot f(y) \forall x, y$. The area (in square units) enclosed by the lines $2|x|+5|y| \leq 4$ expressed interms of $f(1), f(2)$ and $f(4)$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x+y)=f(x) \cdot f(y)$ $\therefore \quad f(x)=a^x$ $f(\mathrm{1})=a^{\mathrm{l}}=2 \Rightarrow a=2$ $\therefore \quad f(x)=2^x$ Area enclosed by the lines $2|x|+5|y| \leq 4$ $4\left(\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times \frac{4}{5}\right)=\frac{16}{5}$
$=\frac{16}{1+(2)^2}=\frac{(2)^4}{1+(2)^2}=\frac{f(4)}{1+f(2)}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha$ is such a minimum value for which the inverse of $f(x)=x^2+3 x-3$ exists in $[\alpha, \infty)$ and $g$ is the inverse of the $f$, then at $x=\alpha+\frac{5}{2}, \frac{d g}{d x}$
questions.solution:
We have, $f(x)=x^2+3 x-3$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=x^2+3 x-3$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=x^2+3 x-3+\frac{9}{4}-\frac{9}{4}$
$\Rightarrow y=\left(x+\frac{3}{2}\right)^2-\frac{21}{4}$
$\Rightarrow \quad y+\frac{21}{4}=\left(x+\frac{3}{2}\right)^2$
$\Rightarrow \quad x+\frac{3}{2}=\sqrt{y+\frac{21}{4}} \Rightarrow x=\sqrt{y+\frac{21}{4}}-\frac{3}{2}$
$\Rightarrow f^{-1}(y)=\sqrt{y+\frac{21}{4}}-\frac{3}{2}$
$\Rightarrow f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{x+\frac{21}{4}}-\frac{3}{2}=g(x)$ (given)
Minimum value of $f^{-1}(x)$ is $\frac{-3}{2}$
$\therefore \quad \alpha=-\frac{3}{2}$
$\therefore \quad x=\alpha+\frac{5}{2}=-\frac{3}{2}+\frac{5}{2}=\frac{2}{2}=1$
Now, $g(x)=\sqrt{x+\frac{21}{4}}-\frac{3}{2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{x+\frac{21}{4}}}$
at $\quad x=1, g^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{1+\frac{21}{4}}}=\frac{1}{5}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of bijective functions $f: \mathbf{Z} \rightarrow \mathbf{Z}$ such that $f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) \forall x, y \in \mathbf{Z}$, is
questions.solution:
Let $x$ and $y$ be any two elements in the domain $(Z)$, such that
$f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)$ $\ldots$ (i)
Differentiating above expression w.r.t ' $y$ ', keeping $x$ constant, we get
$f^{\prime}(x+y)=f^{\prime}(y)$
Let $y=0 \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x+0)=f^{\prime}(0)$
and Assume $f^{\prime}(0)=K$
$\therefore \quad f^{\prime}(x)=K$
Integrating on both sides, we get
$f(x)=K x+C\{C \rightarrow \text { integration constant }\}$ $\ldots$ (ii)
Now putting $x=y=0$ in Eq. (i), we get $f(0+0)=f(0)+f(0) \Rightarrow f(0)=2 f(0)$ or $f(0)=0$
Let $x=0$, from Eq. (ii)
$f(0)=K \times 0+C \text { or } 0=0+C \text { or } C=0$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=k x$
Case (i) If $k>0$, then $f(x)$ is strictly increasing.
Case (ii) If $k < 0$, then $f(x)$ is strictly decreasing So, function is injective Also $f(x)$ where every element in the codomain is a valid output of the function i.e., range is equal to codomain.
So, function is surjective also.
Therefore, there are two bijective functions.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The value of $\Delta \log f(x)+\Delta^{2}\left(3^{x}\right)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \Delta & \log f(x)+\Delta^{2}\left(3^{x}\right) \\ &=\log f(x+h)-\log f(x)+(E-1)^{2} 3^{x} \\ &=\log \left[\frac{f(x+h)}{f(x)}\right]+\left(E^{2}-2 E+1\right) 3^{x} \\ &=\log \left[\frac{E f(x)]}{f(x)}\right]+E^{2}\left(3^{x}\right)-2 E\left(3^{x}\right)+3^{x} \\ &=\log \left[\frac{(1+\Delta) f(x)}{f(x)}\right]+3^{x+2}-2 \cdot 3^{x+1}+3^{x} \\ &=\log \left[1+\frac{\Delta f(x)}{f(x)}\right]+3^{x}(9-6+1) \\ &=\log \left[1+\frac{\Delta f(x)}{f(x)}\right]+4 \cdot 3^{x} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $g$ is the inverse of $f$ and $f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}$, then $g^{\prime}(x)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $g=$ inverse of $f=f^{-1}$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad g(x)=f^{-1}(x)
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad f[g(x)]=x
$$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
f^{\prime}[g(x)] \cdot g^{\prime}(x) &=1 \\
\Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime}(x) &=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}[g(x)]} \\
&=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{1+[g(x)]^{2}}} \\
\Rightarrow & g^{\prime}(x)=1+[g(x)]^{2}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

The graph of $f(x)$ shows that it is a bijective (one-one-onto) function.

questions.q: If <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>k</mi></math> and <math><mi>f</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>g</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> then k =
questions.solution:
We have,
fx=3x+6,gx=4x+k
Since, fogx=gofx
⇒fgx=gfx
⇒f4x+k=g3x+6
⇒34x+k+6=43x+6+K
⇒3k-k=24-6
⇒2k=18
⇒k=9
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\mathrm{f}: \mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}$, such that $f(x)=\frac{e^{x}+e^{-x}}{e^{x}-e^{-x}}$, then $\mathrm{f}$ is
options: a neither even nor odd function
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\frac{e^{x}+e^{-x}}{e^{x}-e^{-x}}$
$=\frac{e^{x}+\frac{1}{e^{x}}}{e^{x}-\frac{1}{e^{x}}}=\frac{e^{2 x}+1}{e^{2 x}-1}$
$f(-x)=\frac{e^{-x}+e^{x}}{e^{-x}-e^{x}}$
$=\frac{\frac{1}{e^{x}}+e^{x}}{\frac{1}{e^{x}}-e^{x}}=\frac{1+e^{2 x}}{1-e^{2 x}}=\frac{1+e^{2 x}}{-\left(e^{2 x}-1\right)}$
$\therefore f(-x)=-f(x)$
$\therefore \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ is an odd function.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If $f(x)=x^{2}-3 x+4$ and $f(x)=f(2 x+1)$, then $x=$
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\text { Here } \mathrm{f}(2 \mathrm{x}+1) & =(2 \mathrm{x}+1)^{2}-3(2 \mathrm{x}+1)+4 \\ & =4 \mathrm{x}^{2}+4 \mathrm{x}+1-6 \mathrm{x}-3+4 \\ \therefore \quad \mathrm{f}(2 \mathrm{x}+1) & =4 \mathrm{x}^{2}-2 \mathrm{x}+2 \\ \text { Given } \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{f}(2 \mathrm{x}+1) \\ \therefore \mathrm{x}^{2}-3 \mathrm{x}+4=4 \mathrm{x}^{2}-2 \mathrm{x}+2 \\ \therefore \quad 3 \mathrm{x}^{2}+\mathrm{x}-2 \quad=0 \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{x}^{2}+3 \mathrm{x}-2 \mathrm{x}-2=0 \\ \quad 3 \mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}+1)-2(\mathrm{x}+1)=0 \Rightarrow(\mathrm{x}+1)(3 \mathrm{x}-2)=0 \\ \therefore \mathrm{x}=-1, \frac{2}{3}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Let f:R→R be a periodic function such that fT+x=1+1-3fx+3fx2-fx313 where T is a fixed positive number, then period of fx is
questions.solution:
Given : fT+x=1+1-fx313

=1+1-fx

⇒fT+x+fx=2 ... (1)

⇒f2T+x+fT+x=2 ... (2)

Subtracting (1) from (2)

f2T+x-fx=0

f2T+x-fx=0

Also, T is positive therefore period of fx=2T
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mn>(</mn><mi>x</mi><mn>)</mn><mo>=</mo><mi>sec</mi><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>log</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></msqrt></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is
options: neither an odd nor an even function
questions.solution:
f −x =sec log −x+ 1+ x 2    

=sec⁡log⁡x+ 1+x2-1  

 ∵1+x2-x= 11+x2+x

=sec −log x+ 1+ x 2    

=seclog x+1+x2     ∵sec−θ=secθ 

=f(x)

Hence f(x) is an even function.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math> <mrow> <mn>2</mn><mtext>f</mtext><mfenced> <mrow> <mtext>xy</mtext></mrow> </mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup> <mrow> <mfenced> <mrow> <mtext>f</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>x</mtext> </mfenced></mrow> </mfenced></mrow> <mtext>y</mtext> </msup> <mo>+</mo><msup> <mrow> <mfenced> <mrow> <mtext>f</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>y</mtext> </mfenced></mrow> </mfenced></mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> </msup> </mrow> </math> for all <math> <mrow> <mtext>x,</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>y</mtext><mo>∈</mo><mtext>R</mtext></mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mtext>f</mtext><mfenced> <mn>1</mn> </mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo></mrow> </math> then the value of <math> <mrow> <munderover> <mi>Σ</mi> <mrow> <mtext>r</mtext> <mo>=</mo> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </munderover> <mtext>f</mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math> is equal to
questions.solution:
From given functional equation,  2f xy = f x y + f y x , ∀x, y∈R

putting y=1 ,

2f x =f x + f 1 x

f x = 3 x

∴     Σ r = 1 1 0 f r = Σ r = 1 1 0 3 r = 3 3 1 0 - 1 3 - 1 = 3 2 3 1 0 - 1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi>x</mi></math> and <math><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is invertible in the domain
questions.solution:
Given, fx=sinx+cosx 

gx=x2-1 

gfx=fx2-1=sinx+cosx2-1=sin2x

But sin2x is invertible in a case when -π2≤2x≤π2

⇒ -π4≤x≤π4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math> <mrow> <mtext>f</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>x</mtext> </mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mtext>x</mtext><mo>−</mo><mi>sin</mi><mtext>x</mtext><mo>,</mo></mrow> </math> <math> <mrow> <mtext>f : R</mtext> <mo> →</mo> <mtext>R</mtext> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>x</mtext> </mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup> <mtext>x</mtext> <mrow> <mfrac> <mn>1</mn> <mn>3</mn> </mfrac> </mrow> </msup> <mo>,</mo></mrow> </math> <math> <mrow> <mtext>g : R</mtext> <mo> →</mo> <mtext>R</mtext> </mrow> </math>, then
questions.solution:
f'x=2-cos x ∀x∈R⇒  one - one,

and g'x=13x-2/3>0⇒one - one

Since, both g and f are one - one, hence, gof is also one - one.

Range of fx is -∞, ∞ as fx is continuous and fx→±∞ as  x→±∞.

Also, since domain of gx is R, y=x13

⇒x=y3

⇒y3∈-∞, ∞

⇒y∈-∞, ∞

⇒ Range of gx is also R.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi></math> is a function defined as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>=</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>−</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi><mo>:</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">∞</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>→</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mfrac><mn>19</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">∞</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>,</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow></math> is its inverse function, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mn>7</mn></mfenced></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
f⁡x=x2-x+5

f'(x)=2x-1
⇒    for y = 7 ,   x > 1 2

∴    7 = x 2 - x + 5

⇒    x 2 - x - 2 = 0

⇒    x - 2 x + 1 = 0

⇒    x = - 1, 2

⇒  x=2 (as x=- 1 is rejected)


If  fx & g x are inverse of each other, gfx=x

⇒g'fx  .f'x=1 

substituting x=2 in this equation,

we get, g'(f(2)).f'(2) = 1

⇒g'(f(2))=1f'(2)
g'(7)=12(2)-1

=13 (at x=2)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The domain of the function <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>9</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
9-x2≠0 and x3-3x>0
x≠-3,3 ....(1)

and  xx2-3>0
⇒xx+3x-3>0

x∈-3,0∪3,∞ …. 2
From 1 ∩ 2, we get,

x∈-3,0∪3,3∪3,∞
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mrow><mo>+</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mrow></math> for all real <math><mi>x</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The least natural number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>n</mi><mi>π</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> for all real values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
fx=sin⁡x3+cos⁡3x10

Period of sin⁡x3=6π

Period of cos⁡3x10=20π3

∵  sin⁡aθ,cos⁡aθ are periodic with period2πa

LCM of 6π,20π3=60π

Therefore, the period of fx=60π

Hence, n=6.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Number of integers in the range of function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><msqrt><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mfenced></math> is (are)
questions.solution:
fx=log2x-12-1+log2(2x-1)2

fx=-log2x-12+log22x-1

fx=-log2x-12+log22x-12

fx=-log2x-12+log22+log2x-12

fx=1, so the range of fx is 1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of solution of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
we know, 0≤cosx≤1 and minimum value of x2+x+2 is −12−4.1.24.1=74

∵ Minimum value of x2+x+2 is greater than the maximum value of cosx.

∴ No common value of x exist for which cosx=x2+x+2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow><mo>+</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></mrow></mrow></mrow></mrow></math> and <math><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math><mi>g</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

fx=sin2⁡x+sin2⁡x+π3+cos⁡xcos⁡x+π3

=sin2⁡x+sin⁡xcos⁡π3+cos⁡xsin⁡π32+cos⁡xcos⁡xcos⁡π3-sin⁡xsin⁡π3

=sin2⁡x+sin⁡x2+cos⁡x.322+cos⁡xcos⁡x2-sin⁡x.32

=sin2⁡x+sin2⁡x4+3cos2⁡x4+sin⁡xcos⁡x. 32+cos2⁡x2-sin⁡xcos⁡32

=5sin2⁡x4+5cos2⁡x4=54

∴   gofx=gfx=g54=1 (given)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi>R</mi><mo>⟶</mo><mi>R</mi></math> be a function defined as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>5</mn><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>5</mn><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>, then the correct option about <math><mi>f</mi></math> is:
questions.solution:
fx=0,for  x≥0152x-1152x+1,for  x<0

∴ fx=1-2152x+1 for all x<0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mn>9</mn><mi>x</mi></msup><mrow><msup><mn>9</mn><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> then find value of 2 times <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>1996</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>1996</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1995</mn><mn>1996</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
We have, fx=9x9x+3

∴ fx+f1-x=1 

∴ f11996+f1-11996=1  ...i 

f21996+f1-21996=1  ...ii

and so on f19951996+f1-19951996=1  ...iii

Adding all the equation we get, 

2 f11996+f21996+…+f19951996=1995

⇒ f11996+f21996+…+f19951996=997.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If [sin−1x]>[cos−1x] where [.] denotes greatest integer function, then the range of ‘x’ contains (also x>0)
options: <math display="inline" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mo form="prefix" stretchy="true">[</mo><mo>sin</mo><mrow><mo form="prefix" stretchy="true">(</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo form="postfix" stretchy="true">)</mo></mrow><mo>,</mo><mi>s</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mrow><mo form="prefix" stretchy="true">(</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo form="postfix" stretchy="true">)</mo></mrow><mo form="postfix" stretchy="true">]</mo></mrow></semantics></math>
questions.solution:
[sin−1x]=0,x∈(0,sin1]

=1,x∈[sin1,1]

[cos−1x]=1,x∈(0,cos1]

=0,x∈(cos1,1]

∴[sin−1x]>[cos−1x]

⇒[sin−1x]=1,[cos−1x]=0

⇒x∈[sin1,1]
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
